name: Remote Terminal (Web Client)
description: Browser-based terminal client with xterm.js that connects to a remote agent via NoLag

topics.name: commands
topics.description: Commands sent from the web client to the remote agent (shell, session_start, session_input, etc.)

topics.name: responses
topics.description: Command responses returned from the agent to the web client

topics.name: status
topics.description: Device status heartbeats broadcast by the agent

topics.name: session_output
topics.description: Interactive PTY session output streamed from the agent to the web client

readme:
# Remote Terminal (Web Client)

## Description
A browser-based terminal emulator that connects to a remote machine through a NoLag-powered agent. Uses xterm.js for terminal rendering and supports full interactive PTY sessions (bash, zsh, PowerShell).

This is the **web client** component of a multi-part system. You also need one of the agent blueprints running on the target machine:
- [remote-terminal](../remote-terminal/) -- Go agent + TUI client
- [remote-terminal-node](../remote-terminal-node/) -- Node.js agent
- [remote-terminal-python](../remote-terminal-python/) -- Python agent

## Getting Started
1. Install dependencies: `npm install`
2. Start the dev server: `npm run dev`
3. Enter your NoLag access token and the target device ID
4. The web client will connect and start an interactive shell session

## Architecture
- **Web client** sends commands on the `commands` topic and receives output on `responses` and `session_output`
- **Agent** (running on target machine) subscribes to `commands`, executes them, and streams output back
- Each device gets its own room (room slug = device ID)
- Interactive sessions use base64-encoded PTY I/O for full terminal emulation

## NoLag Features Used
- **Pub/Sub messaging** for command/response exchange
- **Room-per-device** architecture for multi-device support
- **Reconnection** for resilient connections
- **Binary-safe messaging** via base64 encoding for PTY data
